blocks.body: Advanced passive voice covers complex and less common constructions used in academic, scientific, and formal writing. Here you will learn the passive with infinitives, gerunds, perfect forms, and special cases.

{"level":"C1","intro":"In this topic you will learn advanced passive constructions: the passive with infinitives and gerunds, perfect forms, need/want passive patterns, and stylistic nuances.","rules":[{"label":"Infinitive","color":"emerald","text":"Passive infinitive: to be + V3:","example":"The report needs to be finished."},{"label":"Perfect","color":"blue","text":"Perfect passive: to have been + V3:","example":"He seems to have been promoted."},{"label":"Gerund","color":"rose","text":"Passive gerund: being + V3:","example":"She hates being interrupted."}]}

{"title":"1. Passive Infinitive","sections":[{"label":"To be + V3","color":"emerald","description":"Structure: to be + Past Participle. It is used after modal verbs and certain other verbs.","examples":[{"en":"The report needs to be finished by Friday.","ua":"The report needs to be finished by Friday."},{"en":"I want to be informed immediately.","ua":"I want to be informed immediately."},{"en":"The task is expected to be completed.","ua":"The task is expected to be completed."}]},{"label":"With modal-type verbs","color":"sky","description":"After seem, appear, happen, turn out and some modal-type verbs.","examples":[{"en":"He appears to be well respected.","ua":"He appears to be well respected."},{"en":"The meeting seems to be postponed.","ua":"The meeting seems to be postponed."},{"en":"This document ought to be signed.","ua":"This document ought to be signed."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Perfect Passive Infinitive","sections":[{"label":"To have been + V3","color":"emerald","description":"Structure: to have been + Past Participle. It refers to an action that happened earlier.","examples":[{"en":"He seems to have been promoted.","ua":"He seems to have been promoted."},{"en":"The documents appear to have been lost.","ua":"The documents appear to have been lost."},{"en":"She claims to have been invited.","ua":"She claims to have been invited."}]},{"label":"Use","color":"sky","description":"For assumptions about past events from the present point of view.","examples":[{"en":"He is believed to have been kidnapped.","ua":"He is believed to have been kidnapped."},{"en":"The painting is thought to have been stolen decades ago.","ua":"The painting is thought to have been stolen decades ago."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Passive Gerund","sections":[{"label":"Being + V3","color":"emerald","description":"Structure: being + Past Participle. It is used after prepositions and some verbs.","examples":[{"en":"She hates being interrupted.","ua":"She hates being interrupted."},{"en":"I remember being taught this rule.","ua":"I remember being taught this rule."},{"en":"He denied being involved.","ua":"He denied being involved."}]},{"label":"After prepositions","color":"sky","description":"Often after without, after, before, on, by.","examples":[{"en":"He left without being noticed.","ua":"He left without being noticed."},{"en":"After being rejected, she tried again.","ua":"After being rejected, she tried again."},{"en":"The problem was solved by being discussed openly.","ua":"The problem was solved by being discussed openly."}]}]}

{"title":"4. Perfect Passive Gerund","sections":[{"label":"Having been + V3","color":"emerald","description":"Structure: having been + Past Participle. It is used for actions that happened before others.","examples":[{"en":"Having been warned, he was more careful.","ua":"Having been warned, he was more careful."},{"en":"She regrets having been so rude.","ua":"She regrets having been so rude."},{"en":"Having been told the truth, he felt relieved.","ua":"Having been told the truth, he felt relieved."}]},{"label":"In formal style","color":"sky","description":"It is often used in academic and formal writing.","examples":[{"en":"Having been approved, the project will begin.","ua":"Having been approved, the project will begin."},{"en":"Having been elected, she took office immediately.","ua":"Having been elected, she took office immediately."}]}]}

{"title":"5. Need/Want + Passive Gerund or Infinitive","sections":[{"label":"Need + V-ing (passive meaning)","color":"emerald","description":"Structure: need + V-ing = need to be + V3. It has a passive meaning.","examples":[{"en":"The car needs washing. = The car needs to be washed.","ua":"The car needs washing. = The car needs to be washed."},{"en":"The report needs checking.","ua":"The report needs checking."},{"en":"Your hair needs cutting.","ua":"Your hair needs cutting."}]},{"label":"Want + V-ing (British)","color":"sky","description":"In British English, want + V-ing can also have a passive meaning.","examples":[{"en":"The windows want cleaning. (British)","ua":"The windows want cleaning. (British)"},{"en":"This essay wants rewriting.","ua":"This essay wants rewriting."}]},{"label":"Require + V-ing","color":"amber","description":"Require + V-ing also carries a passive meaning.","examples":[{"en":"The problem requires investigating.","ua":"The problem requires investigating."},{"en":"This matter requires careful handling.","ua":"This matter requires careful handling."}]}]}

{"title":"6. The passive in complex sentences","sections":[{"label":"Relative clauses","color":"emerald","description":"The passive in relative clauses.","examples":[{"en":"The book that was written by Orwell...","ua":"The book that was written by Orwell..."},{"en":"The project which is being developed...","ua":"The project which is being developed..."},{"en":"The issues that have been raised...","ua":"The issues that have been raised..."}]},{"label":"Reduced relative clauses","color":"sky","description":"Reduction with a Past Participle used as a modifier.","examples":[{"en":"The book written by Orwell... (= that was written)","ua":"The book written by Orwell... (= that was written)"},{"en":"The issues raised in the meeting...","ua":"The issues raised in the meeting..."},{"en":"Products made in Italy...","ua":"Products made in Italy..."}]}]}

{"title":"7. Stylistic features of the passive","sections":[{"label":"Scientific style","color":"emerald","description":"In scientific writing, the passive emphasizes objectivity.","examples":[{"en":"The experiment was conducted over six months.","ua":"The experiment was conducted over six months."},{"en":"The data were collected from 500 participants.","ua":"The data were collected from 500 participants."},{"en":"It has been demonstrated that...","ua":"It has been demonstrated that..."}]},{"label":"Formal style","color":"sky","description":"In business and official documents, it adds formality and impersonality.","examples":[{"en":"Your application has been received.","ua":"Your application has been received."},{"en":"Payment is required within 30 days.","ua":"Payment is required within 30 days."},{"en":"Applicants will be notified by email.","ua":"Applicants will be notified by email."}]},{"label":"When NOT to use it","color":"amber","description":"Avoid overusing the passive, because it can make the text harder to process.","examples":[{"en":"❌ The ball was kicked by the player.","ua":"(better: The player kicked the ball.)"},{"en":"❌ Mistakes were made by me.","ua":"(better: I made mistakes.)"}]}]}

{"title":"8. Summary table of advanced forms","intro":"All advanced passive constructions:","rows":[{"en":"Passive Infinitive","ua":"to be + V3","note":"The work needs to be done."},{"en":"Perfect Passive Infinitive","ua":"to have been + V3","note":"He seems to have been promoted."},{"en":"Passive Gerund","ua":"being + V3","note":"She hates being interrupted."},{"en":"Perfect Passive Gerund","ua":"having been + V3","note":"Having been warned, he left."},{"en":"Need + V-ing (passive)","ua":"need + V-ing","note":"The car needs washing."},{"en":"Reduced Relative","ua":"V3 as a modifier","note":"The book written by Orwell..."}]}

{"title":"9. Quick summary","items":["Passive infinitive: to be + V3 — The report needs to be finished.","Perfect passive infinitive: to have been + V3 — He seems to have been promoted.","Passive gerund: being + V3 — She hates being interrupted.","Perfect passive gerund: having been + V3 — Having been warned...","Need/want/require + V-ing can have a passive meaning: The car needs washing.","Reduced relative clauses: V3 used as a modifier — The book written by Orwell.","The passive is often used in scientific and formal writing.","Avoid overusing the passive, because it can make the text heavier."]}
